ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor */,
  "equipment_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the equipment the sensor is attached to */,
  "unit_of_measure" TEXT /* Unit of measure for the sensor data (e.g., temperature, pressure) */,
  "sensor_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the sensor */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of sensor (e.g., temperature, pressure, vibration) */,
  "measurement_range" TEXT /* Valid measurement range for the sensor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "equipment" (
  "equipment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each equipment */,
  "equipment_name" TEXT /* Name of the equipment */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the equipment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the equipment */,
  "equipment_model" TEXT /* Model number of the equipment */,
  "equipment_manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the equipment */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Installation date of the equipment */,
  "decommission_date" TEXT /* Decommission date of the equipment (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("equipment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "time_series_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sensor that collected the data */,
  "timestamps" REAL /* Timestamp of the data point */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the data point */,
  "anomaly" INTEGER /* Anomaly detection flag */,
  "changepoint" INTEGER /* Changepoint detection flag */,
  "trend" INTEGER /* Trend of the data */,
  "noise" REAL /* Noise of the data */,
  "seasonality1" REAL /* Seasonality component 1 */,
  "seasonality2" REAL /* Seasonality component 2 */,
  "seasonality3" REAL /* Seasonality component 3 */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data point (e.g., good, bad, uncertain) */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data point (e.g., sensor, user input) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_time_series_data_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "anomaly_detection" (
  "anomaly_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each anomaly */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sensor that detected the anomaly */,
  "anomaly_score" REAL /* Anomaly score */,
  "anomaly_type" TEXT /* Type of anomaly (e.g., high, low, spike) */,
  "anomaly_timestamp" REAL /* Timestamp of the anomaly */,
  "anomaly_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the anomaly (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "anomaly_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the anomaly */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("anomaly_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_anomaly_detection_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "changepoint_detection" (
  "changepoint_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each changepoint */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sensor that detected the changepoint */,
  "changepoint_score" REAL /* Changepoint score */,
  "changepoint_type" TEXT /* Type of changepoint (e.g., increase, decrease) */,
  "changepoint_timestamp" REAL /* Timestamp of the changepoint */,
  "changepoint_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the changepoint (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "changepoint_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the changepoint */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("changepoint_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_changepoint_detection_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "trend_analysis" (
  "trend_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each trend */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sensor that detected the trend */,
  "trend_score" REAL /* Trend score */,
  "trend_type" TEXT /* Type of trend (e.g., increasing, decreasing) */,
  "trend_timestamp" REAL /* Timestamp of the trend */,
  "trend_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the trend (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "trend_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the trend */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trend_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_trend_analysis_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "seasonality_analysis" (
  "seasonality_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each seasonality */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sensor that detected the seasonality */,
  "seasonality_score" REAL /* Seasonality score */,
  "seasonality_type" TEXT /* Type of seasonality (e.g., daily, weekly) */,
  "seasonality_timestamp" REAL /* Timestamp of the seasonality */,
  "seasonality_period" TEXT /* Period of the seasonality */,
  "seasonality_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the seasonality */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("seasonality_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_seasonality_analysis_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "noise_analysis" (
  "noise_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each noise */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sensor that detected the noise */,
  "noise_score" REAL /* Noise score */,
  "noise_type" TEXT /* Type of noise (e.g., high, low) */,
  "noise_timestamp" REAL /* Timestamp of the noise */,
  "noise_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the noise (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "noise_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the noise */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("noise_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_noise_analysis_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, operator, analyst) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who accessed the system */,
  "access_timestamp" REAL /* Timestamp of the access event */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., login, logout) */,
  "access_location" TEXT /* Location of the access event (e.g., IP address, GPS coordinates) */,
  "access_device" TEXT /* Device used for the access event (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_records" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance event */,
  "equipment_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the equipment that was maintained */,
  "maintenance_timestamp" REAL /* Timestamp of the maintenance event */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance (e.g., routine, corrective) */,
  "maintenance_description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance event */,
  "maintenance_category" TEXT /* Category of the maintenance event (e.g., routine, corrective) */,
  "maintenance_priority" TEXT /* Priority of the maintenance event (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_records_equipment_id FOREIGN KEY ("equipment_id") REFERENCES equipment ("equipment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who received the notification */,
  "notification_timestamp" REAL /* Timestamp of the notification */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., alert, warning) */,
  "notification_message" TEXT /* Message of the notification */,
  "notification_category" TEXT /* Category of the notification (e.g., alert, warning) */,
  "notification_priority" TEXT /* Priority of the notification (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "thresholds" (
  "threshold_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each threshold */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sensor that the threshold applies to */,
  "threshold_value" REAL /* Value of the threshold */,
  "threshold_type" TEXT /* Type of threshold (e.g., high, low) */,
  "threshold_category" TEXT /* Category of the threshold (e.g., high, low) */,
  "threshold_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the threshold */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("threshold_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_thresholds_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)
